title: NOC 13110 – Administrative Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Administrative Assistants play a critical role in keeping Canadian workplaces organized, efficient, and compliant. Classified under NOC 13110, this occupation remains in strong demand nationwide and provides realistic immigration opportunities through skilled worker programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-supported work permits.

classification.teer: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training
classification.minor_group: 1311 – Office administrative assistants – general, legal, and medical

common_job_titles: Administrative assistant Executive secretary (except legal and medical) Human resources administrator Office administrative assistant Private secretary Secretary (except legal and medical) Technical secretary
duties: Prepare, edit, and proofread correspondence, reports, invoices, and presentations
Distribute physical and electronic mail and coordinate information flow
Order office supplies and maintain inventory
Answer calls and respond to electronic inquiries
Greet visitors and direct them appropriately
employment_requirements: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training
job_outlook: From 2024 to 2033, Canada is projected to create 110,200 new positions for Administrative Assistants, with 109,200 job seekers expected—making strategic province selection and professional immigration planning especially important for overseas candidates.
benefits: Paid maternity, parental, and caregiver leave
Employer contributions to Employment Insurance (EI) and Canada Pension Plan (CPP) or Quebec Pension Plan (QPP)
additional_information: Administrative Assistants are employed across corporate offices, government agencies, schools, hospitals, real estate firms, logistics companies, and private enterprises.

Post-pandemic hiring growth across provincesStable office-based and hybrid rolesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported work permit optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $58,412 to $69,223 CAD
Most professionals in this occupation work 35–40 hours per week, providing predictable schedules and long-term stability.
Why Choose Canada in This Occupation?

Universal public healthcareStrong labour protectionsCompetitive salaries and benefitsSecure employment environmentsMulticultural workplacesWork–life balanceClear pathways from work permits to Permanent Residence
Administrative Assistants form the operational backbone of Canadian organizations, making this role consistently in demand from coast to coast.
